name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #1
description: Florentine Groteschi #1 is a glitch candy rot single-panel relic built around Medici and 404 Error Reliquary. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Re di Coppe, used here as the witness card. A rizzler reliquary custody dispute gets pulled into a codon decoder sticker on a cold wallet, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. The small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks. The generated insert, a flaming auction paddle shaped like a hardware wallet, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks FICINO at Σ 336.

attributes.value: a flaming auction paddle shaped like a hardware wallet

florentine_groteschi.collector_note: Custom top-level lore data: not an OpenSea trait list, but intentionally readable in the raw token JSON.

chess_piece.name: Pawn

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.history_now_bridge: WET FRESCO INCIDENT: a rizzler reliquary custody dispute gets pulled into a codon decoder sticker on a cold wallet. Re di Coppe reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. The small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks.

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a flaming auction paddle shaped like a hardware wallet

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Medici anchors the historical source. Re di Coppe supplies the witness logic. The flaming auction paddle shaped like a hardware wallet acts as the modern meme-object. The glitch candy rot palette and 404 Error Reliquary styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Medici is treated as evidence in a wet fresco incident. The old-world clue is a rizzler reliquary custody dispute; the modern-world echo is a codon decoder sticker on a cold wallet. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Re di Coppe card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. The small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.
